nodes.label: A-1 - Rise coinciding with failed synthesis suppression against a compensating majority implies a feedback-control responder mechanism
nodes.title: The plasma-cholesterol rise appearing specifically where synthesis suppression failed, set against a 69% compensating majority, supports a real feedback-control buffering mechanism (rather than mere random variation) operating through absorption/synthesis; it is only weakly consistent with — and does not on its own establish — a stable individualized responder axis, since the accounting is per diet-period rather than per subject.

nodes.label: A-10 - US-only egg-T2D signal plus null after cholesterol-protein adjustment supports eggs marking a dietary pattern rather than causing T2D
nodes.title: The T2D association appearing only in US studies (internally homogeneous at 1.18) but not in non-US studies, combined with the finding that adjusting for eggs' cholesterol and protein content did not materially change the COSM estimate, is better explained by egg intake marking the surrounding dietary pattern than by a nutrient-mediated causal effect of eggs.

nodes.label: A-11 - Germ-free-antibiotic necessity plus TMAO-supplementation sufficiency plus host-genetic segregation jointly establish a causal choline--flora--TMAO--atherosclerosis chain in mice
nodes.title: The orthogonal mouse lines — gut flora necessary (germ-free/antibiotic abolition), TMAO supplementation sufficient (reproduces atherosclerosis without lipid change), and host FMO3 genetics co-segregating with plaque and TMAO — together establish a causal chain from dietary choline through gut flora and TMAO to atherosclerosis, not a mere correlation.

nodes.label: A-12 - Human TMAO-CVD evidence is observational in a selected angiography cohort, so it supports but does not establish human causality
nodes.title: The human choline/TMAO-CVD associations, though adjusted and partly prospective, rest on a referral-selected high-risk angiography cohort and cannot by themselves establish human causality; they raise the probability of a causal human effect mainly by riding on the strong mouse mechanism.

nodes.label: A-13 - Isocaloric substitution algebra makes the egg effect the difference of two food coefficients, so no unconditional egg effect is identified
nodes.title: Because a substitution HR is the contrast between two foods' coefficients under a constrained total, the estimated effect of eggs is definitionally comparator-relative, so this design identifies no single unconditional egg effect.

nodes.label: A-14 - Full-serving-per-day substitution benefits apply only to the small high-intake minority, so population impact of cutting eggs is smaller
nodes.title: Because a one-serving/day substitution presupposes at least one serving/day of current intake, the large per-day risk reductions apply only to the ~8% eating that many eggs, so the population-average benefit of reducing eggs is much smaller than the per-day HRs suggest.

nodes.label: A-15 - Observed egg LDL-C change was far below the dietary-cholesterol dose-response prediction, consistent with yolk phospholipids blunting absorption
nodes.title: Standard dietary-cholesterol response equations predict a +232 mg/day cholesterol increase would raise LDL-C by ~7.4 mg/dL, yet the between-condition LDL-C change was -3.5 mg/dL — a ~10.9 mg/dL shortfall against prediction; because that difference also reflects the high-carb comparator's own LDL effect, the shortfall is weakly consistent with — not a demonstration of — an egg-specific mechanism (proposed: yolk phospholipids, ~1.75 g/yolk) blunting the cholesterol-raising effect.

nodes.label: A-16 - Crude-to-adjusted reversal shows the egg-CVD association is confounded by an unhealthy-eater pattern
nodes.title: The crude age-adjusted egg-CVD association (HR 1.10) reversing to null (0.93) only after adjustment for co-consumed foods and lifestyle shows the raw association is confounded by an unhealthy dietary/lifestyle pattern; the same correlation structure means residual confounding could still bias the adjusted estimate in either direction.

nodes.label: A-17 - E-value- confounding of RR-=1.43 would be needed to turn the egg-CVD null positive
nodes.title: E-value analysis shows an unmeasured confounder would need to be associated with both egg intake and CVD by a risk ratio of at least ~1.43-fold to move the adjusted confidence interval to a positive association (or ~1.28-fold toward an inverse one), so only fairly strong unmeasured confounding could overturn the null.

nodes.label: A-18 - Region-structured heterogeneity favors population effect-modification over a universal egg-CVD null
nodes.title: The pooled null (I2=62.3%) masks region-specific estimates whose heterogeneity is largely explained by geography (Asia inverse 0.92, US null 1.01, Europe null 1.05), so the data are poorly described by a single homogeneous effect and are consistent with population/dietary-context effect-modification — but region-correlated cohort artifacts (measurement, background diet, confounding, healthcare) explain the same structure equally well, so modification is suggested, not favoured over a null-plus-differential-bias account.

nodes.label: A-19 - Self-reported T2D over a short 5-year follow-up biases toward the null, so the JPHC egg null under-discriminates
nodes.title: Because incident T2D was self-reported over only ~5 years, outcome under-ascertainment and non-differential misclassification bias hazard estimates toward the null, so the observed egg-T2D null is consistent both with a true absence of effect and with a modest positive effect masked by low power and measurement error.

nodes.label: A-2 - A registered RCT null in the exact at-risk population causally counterweights the cohort harm signal (with a background-diet-match caveat)
nodes.title: A pre-registered RCT returning a tight null on lipids and glycemia in exactly the prediabetes/T2D population where observational cohorts flag egg-associated harm is strong causal counter-evidence against that harm signal — though the matched background diet caps how strongly the null can be read.

nodes.label: A-20 - The closed-form E-value formula shows small cohort hazard ratios need only modest unmeasured confounding to be explained away
nodes.title: The closed-form E-value formula quantifies exactly how strong the joint confounder–exposure and confounder–outcome associations must be to reduce an observed risk ratio to the null, and applied to typical cohort magnitudes it shows small hazard ratios require only modest unmeasured confounding to be explained away, larger ones require strong confounding.

nodes.label: A-21 - Egg association vanishing on cholesterol adjustment implies dietary cholesterol is the operative exposure
nodes.title: That the egg association loses significance after adjusting for dietary cholesterol, while dietary cholesterol stays significantly and monotonically associated, indicates dietary cholesterol statistically accounts for (mediates/confounds) the egg-CVD and egg-mortality associations -- supporting cholesterol content, not egg per se, as the operative exposure; though near-collinearity of egg and dietary-cholesterol intake limits clean separation.

nodes.label: A-22 - Choline was absorbed in the egg arm yet TMAO did not rise, isolating choline form (not dose or compliance) as the TMAO determinant
nodes.title: Because plasma choline rose in the egg arm (choline was ingested and absorbed) yet TMAO did not, while an equivalent free-choline dose raised TMAO ~6-fold, the egg TMAO null cannot be attributed to under-dosing or non-compliance and instead isolates the chemical form/bioavailability of choline as the determinant of TMAO generation.

nodes.label: A-23 - Because TMAO is cleared almost exclusively by the kidney, the normal-renal egg-null cannot be extrapolated to renal impairment
nodes.title: Since TMAO is eliminated almost entirely by renal excretion, an egg/choline null observed in participants pre-selected for normal renal function cannot be generalized to people with impaired renal function, in whom reduced clearance could allow TMAO to accumulate even from food-matrix choline.

nodes.label: A-24 - Adverse egg-CVD HR confined to the diabetic stratum supports effect-modification by diabetes, but the small subgroup makes it imprecise
nodes.title: That the elevated egg-CVD hazard appears only in the diabetic stratum (HR 2.81) and is absent in non-diabetics (1.03) and the whole cohort (1.14, null) is the basis for inferring effect-modification by diabetes status; but because the diabetic estimate rests on 79 cases with a wide CI whose lower bound (1.25) is near 1, the interaction is plausible yet imprecise and may overstate the true effect size.

nodes.label: A-25 - Implausible, mutually incompatible implied effects mark the cohort estimates as bias-driven not causal
nodes.title: Because the meta-analyzed cohort estimates imply individually implausible and jointly incompatible life-expectancy effects — a picture made worse, not rescued, by the attenuation defense — they cannot be read at face value as literal lifelong causal effects and are strongly distorted (by cumulative bias and/or invalid lifelong-linear extrapolation).

nodes.label: A-26 - Universal correlation among dietary variables plus unmeasured behavioral confounding and flexible analysis make single-food effects non-identifiable in cohorts
nodes.title: Because almost all dietary variables are mutually correlated and diet co-varies with many unmeasured, time-varying behavioral/social factors that no cohort fully captures, a single food's causal effect cannot be isolated; near-universal statistical significance is what one expects under pervasive confounding plus flexible, non-prespecified analysis, not evidence of causation.

nodes.label: A-27 - The parallel ~10% apoB rise means added egg cholesterol raises atherogenic LDL-particle number, not just cholesterol per particle
nodes.title: Because apolipoprotein B rose ~10% in step with the LDL/total-cholesterol increase, the added egg cholesterol raised the number of circulating apoB-lipoprotein particles, not merely the cholesterol carried per particle — making the lipid shift atherogenically meaningful rather than cosmetic.

nodes.label: A-28 - Small, CI-crossing genotype effects for fat and cafestol and none for dietary cholesterol imply APOE genotyping has little practical value for identifying diet-responders
nodes.title: Even the genotype effects that do appear (saturated fat +0.08 mmol/L, cafestol -0.11 mmol/L) are small, point in opposite directions, and have 95% CIs that cross zero, while the dietary-cholesterol challenge shows no genotype difference at all — so APOE genotype by itself carries too little discriminating information to identify diet-responsive individuals.

nodes.label: A-29 - FFQ under-reporting is large and BMI-correlated, violating the classical measurement-error model that standard attenuation correction assumes
nodes.title: Because the FFQ's under-reporting is both large and includes a systematic, person-specific component correlated with BMI (and with true intake), the classical assumption of non-differential additive error is violated, so FFQ-derived diet exposures carry person-level bias that a single attenuation/regression-calibration factor cannot fully remove.

nodes.label: A-3 - Haemorrhagic-stroke-driven benefit limits transfer of China Kadoorie protective signal to Western populations
nodes.title: Because the aggregate egg-CVD HR is a composition-weighted mixture of endpoint-specific HRs, it is population-specific and not directly portable; the haemorrhagic-stroke-specific protection (0.74, the largest endpoint effect) applies to a smaller CVD share in the West — but since coronary disease (the dominant Western endpoint) is itself protective at 0.86-0.88, this does not establish that overall egg-CVD benefit moves toward null in Western populations, only that the aggregate figure should not be transferred as-is.

nodes.label: A-30 - High event count makes PURE's tight null informative against a moderate harm or benefit of eggs
nodes.title: Because PURE accrued over 14,000 composite events, its CI (0.89-1.04) is a precise, well-powered null rather than an underpowered non-finding: it is informative against moderate harm (upper bound ~1.04) and against benefit larger than ~11%, though a modest benefit up to ~11% remains consistent — and the bound is on random error in the observed association, not on a true causal effect if systematic bias (confounding, non-classical FFQ error) is present.

nodes.label: A-31 - Absence of any adverse lipid change removes the main a-priori pathway for egg harm, making the null mechanistically coherent
nodes.title: Because higher egg intake produced no rise in LDL or total cholesterol (and lower blood pressure), the dietary-cholesterol-to-lipids-to-CVD pathway that motivates limiting eggs is not engaged here, so the null clinical result is mechanistically coherent rather than a harm masked by confounding.

nodes.label: A-32 - Isolated MI and prevalent-CVD-subgroup signals are best read as chance given multiplicity and non-replication
nodes.title: The two nominally significant egg-protective signals in PURE - lower MI and lower composite risk in prevalent-CVD participants - are most likely chance, because both fail to replicate in ONTARGET/TRANSCEND, the subgroup interaction is non-significant, and many endpoints were tested.

nodes.label: A-33 - Reversible depletion plus liver dysfunction on choline removal from an otherwise-complete diet meets the criterion for essentiality
nodes.title: That removing choline from an otherwise nutritionally-complete diet (adequate methionine/folate) produced tissue choline depletion and reversible liver dysfunction, both absent in the choline-continued control and both reversing on repletion, satisfies the classical criterion for an essential nutrient - so despite endogenous synthesis, dietary choline is required in humans.

nodes.label: A-34 - HDL rose while LDL and the LDL-HDL ratio held across absorber phenotypes, so the egg lipid effect is neutral-to-favorable rather than atherogenic
nodes.title: Because HDL-C rose while LDL-C stayed flat, the LDL:HDL ratio was preserved rather than worsened; and because this preservation held in both hyper- and hypo-absorbers of dietary cholesterol, the egg-induced HDL gain is not bought at the cost of a more atherogenic LDL profile in any absorber subgroup — so the average whole-egg lipid effect here is neutral-to-favorable, not harmful.

nodes.label: A-35 - Non-differential, calibratable dietary error biases against not toward positive findings, so measurement error is correctable rather than fatal
nodes.title: Because dietary under-reporting is largely non-differential — which attenuates a dichotomous exposure–disease association toward the null and, for graded/energy-adjusted exposures, usually (though not provably always) attenuates rather than inflates it — and because its magnitude is estimable from biomarker-validation studies, energy adjustment and regression/biomarker calibration can recover de-attenuated estimates; so non-differential measurement error usually weakens rather than manufactures positive diet–disease associations and is a bounded, correctable, not fatal, problem.

nodes.label: A-36 - Replication across cohorts with different confounding structures plus sensitivity analysis makes residual confounding an unlikely and bounded explanation
nodes.title: A diet–disease association that replicates across independent cohorts whose confounding structures differ is unlikely to be produced by a single shared unmeasured confounder, and sensitivity analysis bounds how strong such a confounder would have to be — so residual confounding is a quantifiable and often implausible explanation rather than an automatic disqualifier.

nodes.label: A-37 - Triangulating cohorts with mechanism and intermediate-endpoint trials supports causal inference for policy given hard-endpoint diet RCTs are often infeasible
nodes.title: When prospective-cohort findings converge with biological mechanism, biomarker studies, and randomized trials of intermediate endpoints (satisfying Bradford-Hill criteria), they jointly strengthen causal inference and — because hard-endpoint dietary RCTs are frequently infeasible, unblindable, and undermined by poor compliance and dropout — constitute the best available basis for policy; but this convergence licenses policy-adequate confidence only where the corroborating mechanistic/trial evidence is itself strong and concordant, and convergence on intermediate endpoints does not by itself guarantee the hard-endpoint effect.

nodes.label: A-38 - Reverse causation from subclinical disease is removable by prospective design plus early-follow-up exclusion and lag analyses
nodes.title: Reverse causation — subclinical disease altering diet or an exposure before diagnosis — is addressable in prospective cohorts by excluding events in the first years of follow-up and by lagged analyses, which restores reasonably unbiased estimates, so it is a handled design/analysis issue rather than an intractable one.

nodes.label: A-39 - The -=2-yolk threshold and 4-6 yolk plateau imply saturating conversion capacity, bounding TMAO exposure from high egg intake
nodes.title: The significance threshold near 2 yolks and the plateau of urinary TMAO between 4 and 6 yolks indicate the choline-to-TMAO conversion capacity saturates, so each additional egg beyond a few adds progressively less TMAO — placing an upper bound on how much habitual high egg intake can raise TMAO exposure.

nodes.label: A-4 - Identical eczema treatment in both arms isolates the causal effect of egg introduction, not eczema management
nodes.title: Because both trial arms received identical aggressive eczema treatment and differed only in egg versus placebo powder, the ~30-percentage-point allergy reduction is attributable to egg introduction itself, not to eczema/skin-barrier management.

nodes.label: A-40 - A postprandial TMAO spike with unchanged inflammation-oxidation markers does not establish that egg-driven TMAO causes harm
nodes.title: Because the measured rise is a single-meal postprandial spike against a low-choline background, not a fasting steady-state increase, and because the two downstream harm surrogates (hsCRP, oxidized LDL) did not move, this study shows eggs raise TMAO flux but does not establish that egg-driven TMAO produces cardiovascular harm.

nodes.label: A-41 - Elevated risk at both intake extremes within one cohort refutes any monotone egg-CVD dose-response for this population
nodes.title: Because risk is higher at both very low and very high intake than at 3-<6 eggs/week, the association cannot be captured by any single monotone (always-protective or always-harmful) dose-response in this cohort.

nodes.label: A-42 - The low-intake arm of the U may overstate harm because very low egg intake marks low socioeconomic status and poor health
nodes.title: The excess risk in the <1 egg/week group is liable to inflation by reverse causation and confounding by low socioeconomic status, so the low-intake side of the U-shape probably overstates any causal harm of eating few eggs.

nodes.label: A-5 - Region- and diet-quality-dependence plus incomplete attenuation point to the US egg-T2D signal being confounded by the Western dietary pattern
nodes.title: The concentration of the egg-T2D association in the US (region p-interaction 0.01) and at low overall diet quality (AHEI p-interaction 0.03), together with its persistence after adjusting for co-consumed foods, is more consistent with confounding by the Western dietary pattern eggs mark than with a universal causal effect of eggs.

nodes.label: A-6 - Substitution pattern - eggs worse than plant foods and dairy but equivalent to other animal proteins - fits eggs as an animal-food marker rather than a unique cause
nodes.title: That swapping eggs for whole grains, nuts or dairy lowers modelled T2D risk while swapping eggs for other animal proteins (meat, poultry, fish) shows no significant change is consistent with eggs being no more diabetogenic than the animal-food class they belong to, and supports (without establishing) the association being driven by the broader dietary pattern rather than something specific to eggs.

nodes.label: A-7 - Illness from intact refrigerated Grade A eggs excludes shell contamination and implies internal trans-ovarian origin
nodes.title: Because outbreaks occurred despite intact, uncracked, properly-refrigerated eggs, the usual external contamination routes (cracked/soiled shells, surface contamination, post-lay mishandling) are strongly disfavoured, making an internal pre-shell (trans-ovarian) origin the best-supported explanation.

nodes.label: A-8 - Serotype-specific egg association (SE 44% vs 15% others) points to eggs as the SE-specific vehicle
nodes.title: The national contrast -- SE outbreaks egg-associated 44% of the time versus 15% for other Salmonella serotypes -- indicates the egg link is specific to the enteritidis serotype, supporting eggs as the SE-specific vehicle rather than a generic Salmonella-egg association.

nodes.label: A-9 - Keys square-root dose-response equation makes the dietary-cholesterol effect concave (diminishing returns)
nodes.title: The least-squares relation ΔSerum-cholesterol = 1.5·(√C2 − √C1), with C in mg cholesterol/1000 kcal, makes serum-cholesterol response a square-root (concave) function of intake, so each additional unit of dietary cholesterol raises serum cholesterol less at higher baseline intakes; at habitual intakes the marginal effect is therefore small.

nodes.label: H-1 - Plasma-cholesterol response to dietary cholesterol is highly individualized (hypo- vs hyper-responders)
nodes.title: The plasma-cholesterol response to dietary cholesterol is highly individualized: most people are hypo-responders whose feedback control buffers added dietary cholesterol, while a minority (on the order of 31%) are hyper-responders who show a meaningful plasma-cholesterol rise.

nodes.label: H-10 - Early stepwise egg introduction causally prevents egg allergy in high-risk infants
nodes.title: Early, controlled stepwise introduction of heated egg from ~6 months of age (with concurrent eczema control) causally prevents development of hen's egg allergy in high-risk infants with eczema, rather than merely being safe — i.e. oral tolerance induction actively reduces allergy incidence.

nodes.label: H-11 - Trans-ovarian (internal, pre-shell) contamination, not shell contamination, drives the SE egg epidemic
nodes.title: The SE egg epidemic arises from trans-ovarian (internal, pre-shell) contamination -- the pathogen is deposited inside the egg via the hen's reproductive tract before shell formation -- rather than from surface/shell contamination of cracked or soiled eggs.

nodes.label: H-12 - Intact Grade A shell eggs are a major vehicle of the US Salmonella enteritidis epidemic
nodes.title: Internally-contaminated intact Grade A shell eggs are a major vehicle driving the epidemic rise in human Salmonella enteritidis infection in the US -- eggs are a leading food source of this serotype's illness burden.

nodes.label: H-15 - Gut flora convert dietary phosphatidylcholine-choline to TMAO, which causally promotes atherosclerosis independent of lipids
nodes.title: Dietary phosphatidylcholine/choline is metabolized by gut flora to TMAO, which causally promotes atherosclerosis by upregulating macrophage scavenger receptors and foam-cell formation — a lipid-independent, microbiota-dependent atherogenic pathway.

nodes.label: H-16 - Elevated plasma TMAO is a clinically meaningful (causal) cardiovascular risk factor in humans
nodes.title: Elevated plasma TMAO is a clinically meaningful, plausibly causal cardiovascular risk factor in humans, not merely a passive marker.

nodes.label: H-17 - Eggs are a relatively unhealthy protein source; benefit comes from replacing eggs with plant or fish protein
nodes.title: Eggs are among the least-healthy protein sources for CVD and mortality (comparable to processed meat, worse than fish, poultry, nuts, legumes and whole grains); dietary benefit accrues from replacing eggs with plant or fish protein rather than from any property of eggs consumed in isolation.

nodes.label: H-18 - The egg-health effect is a property of the comparator not of eggs alone, so eggs good-or-bad is ill-posed
nodes.title: The health effect attributed to eggs is not a fixed property of egg intake but of the food eggs displace; an unconditional verdict that eggs are beneficial, harmful, or neutral is not well-defined without specifying the substitution.

nodes.label: H-19 - Substituting eggs for high-carb breakfast foods improves or does not worsen glycemic-insulin outcomes in adults at risk for T2D
nodes.title: In adults with metabolic syndrome/prediabetes, replacing energy-matched higher-carbohydrate (higher-sugar) breakfast foods with ~2 eggs/day improves, or at least does not worsen, insulin sensitivity and other glycemic markers.

nodes.label: H-2 - Feedback down-regulation of absorption and synthesis buffers dietary cholesterol in most people
nodes.title: Most people avoid a plasma-cholesterol rise from dietary cholesterol because a feedback system down-regulates fractional cholesterol absorption and/or endogenous synthesis; hyper-responders' rise reflects a failure of that synthesis suppression.

nodes.label: H-20 - In dysglycemic adults, replacing high-carb breakfast foods with eggs modestly worsens LDL-C
nodes.title: In adults with metabolic syndrome/prediabetes, substituting ~2 eggs/day for energy-matched higher-carbohydrate breakfast foods produces a modestly less favorable LDL-C trajectory (a small adverse lipid effect relative to the high-carb comparator).

nodes.label: H-21 - Moderate egg intake has no causal effect on CVD risk overall
nodes.title: Moderate egg consumption (up to ~1 egg/day) has no causal effect on cardiovascular disease or all-cause mortality risk in the general adult population, a neutrality that holds across low-, middle- and high-income populations.

nodes.label: H-22 - Region-population modifies the egg-CVD association (inverse in Asia, null in West)
nodes.title: The egg-CVD association is modified by population/region -- inverse (protective) in Asian populations but null in US and European populations -- reflecting differences in dietary context and what eggs are eaten with or displace.

nodes.label: H-23 - Egg's CVD impact is relative to the food it replaces (neutral vs healthy proteins, better than red-processed meat)
nodes.title: Whether egg consumption is cardiovascularly beneficial, neutral, or harmful depends on the food it displaces: eggs are neutral relative to fish, poultry, and plant proteins but preferable to red/processed meat or full-fat milk.

nodes.label: H-24 - Egg consumption raises CVD risk specifically in people with type 2 diabetes
nodes.title: Egg consumption raises cardiovascular disease risk specifically among people with type 2 diabetes, even if it is neutral in the general population.

nodes.label: H-26 - Dietary cholesterol content, not egg per se, is the operative exposure behind the egg-CVD association
nodes.title: The dietary cholesterol content of eggs, not eggs per se, is the operative exposure driving the observed egg-CVD and egg-mortality associations; eggs matter for cardiovascular risk only via the cholesterol they contribute.

nodes.label: H-27 - Higher dietary cholesterol - egg intake causally raises CVD and mortality risk (dose-response)
nodes.title: Higher dietary cholesterol and (via its cholesterol) higher egg consumption causally increase incident cardiovascular disease and all-cause mortality risk in US adults, in a dose-dependent manner without an apparent threshold.

nodes.label: H-28 - The form-food-matrix of choline, not total choline content, determines TMAO generation
nodes.title: The chemical form of dietary choline (free choline vs choline bound as phosphatidylcholine, including egg-yolk choline), not the total amount of choline ingested, determines gut-microbial TMA production and systemic TMAO; phosphatidylcholine is less bioavailable for TMA generation than free choline.

nodes.label: H-29 - Habitual whole-egg consumption does not meaningfully raise TMAO in people with normal renal function
nodes.title: Habitual whole-egg consumption (up to ~4 eggs/day) does not meaningfully raise fasting TMAO - a proposed cardiovascular-harm mediator - in people with normal renal function, undercutting a TMAO-mediated harm pathway for eggs specifically.

nodes.label: H-3 - Dietary fat quality is a larger determinant of plasma cholesterol than dietary cholesterol quantity
nodes.title: Dietary fat quality (degree of saturation) is a larger determinant of plasma cholesterol than the amount of dietary cholesterol consumed.

nodes.label: H-30 - Impaired renal function may modify the TMAO response to egg-choline intake
nodes.title: In people with impaired renal function, egg or choline intake may raise TMAO (unlike in those with normal renal function), because TMAO is cleared almost exclusively by the kidney and could accumulate when clearance is reduced.

nodes.label: H-31 - Nutritional-cohort diet-disease associations reflect cumulative bias and cannot support causal dietary claims
nodes.title: The diet–mortality/CVD associations produced by observational nutritional-epidemiology cohorts reflect almost exclusively cumulative bias — extensive residual confounding among near-universally correlated dietary exposures, unmeasured time-varying behavioral/social factors, error-laden self-reported intake, and selective/non-prespecified analysis — rather than causal effects, so these cohorts cannot support the causal dietary claims (including about eggs) routinely drawn from them and folded into guidelines.

nodes.label: H-33 - Impaired insulin sensitivity in T2DM deranges cholesterol transport, making egg-derived dietary cholesterol more atherogenic in diabetics
nodes.title: The diabetic-specific egg harm arises because low insulin sensitivity in T2DM raises total/VLDL/LDL cholesterol and alters cholesterol transport (decreased apolipoprotein E, increased apolipoprotein C3), so the extra dietary cholesterol delivered by eggs is handled abnormally and is more atherogenic in diabetics than in non-diabetics.

nodes.label: H-34 - Egg-derived dietary cholesterol raises fasting LDL-total cholesterol linearly, roughly +1.4-1.5 mg-dL per 100 mg
nodes.title: Dietary cholesterol from eggs raises fasting total and LDL cholesterol in a linear, dose-dependent way — on the order of +1.4 to 1.5 mg/dL per 100 mg cholesterol — with the rise borne by LDL (and apoB particle number) rather than HDL, at least in healthy young men.

nodes.label: H-35 - Cholesterol responsiveness to dietary cholesterol is a continuous (normally distributed) trait, not a discrete responder-non-responder split
nodes.title: Individual sensitivity of plasma cholesterol to dietary cholesterol is a continuously (approximately normally) distributed quantitative trait rather than a discrete hypo- vs hyper-responder dichotomy.

nodes.label: H-36 - APOE genotype by itself does not identify dietary-cholesterol hyper-responders
nodes.title: APOE genotype (in particular E4 carriage) does not by itself identify who hyper-responds to dietary cholesterol; the popular 'ApoE4 carriers hyper-respond to dietary cholesterol' claim does not hold for the dietary-cholesterol challenge specifically.

nodes.label: H-38 - The health effect of eggs depends on background diet, being least harmful when displacing refined carbohydrate
nodes.title: The cardiovascular effect of eggs is contingent on the background diet: eggs are least likely to be harmful (and possibly beneficial) when they displace refined carbohydrate in high-carbohydrate, lower-quality diets (e.g. China, South Asia, Africa, low-income populations), which could reconcile protective Asian and null/harmful Western findings.

nodes.label: H-39 - Choline is an essential nutrient for humans when dietary methionine and folate are not in excess
nodes.title: Dietary choline is essential for humans (not merely synthesizable de novo): when methionine and folate are adequate but not in excess, endogenous synthesis is insufficient, so a choline-free diet produces deficiency signs and dietary choline is required.

nodes.label: H-4 - High egg intake does not harm blood lipids or glycemic control in people with prediabetes-T2D
nodes.title: In people with prediabetes or type 2 diabetes, a high egg intake (~12 eggs/week) does not adversely affect blood lipids or glycemic control, i.e. eggs are cardiometabolically safe at this intake in this at-risk group (at least over ~3 months).

nodes.label: H-40 - Whole eggs on a carbohydrate-restricted diet produce a net-favorable-neutral lipid response (HDL up, LDL-HDL preserved) even in hyper-absorbers
nodes.title: Adding whole eggs to a carbohydrate-restricted diet produces a net-beneficial-to-neutral blood-lipid response - raising HDL-C while leaving LDL-C and the LDL:HDL ratio unchanged - and this holds even among dietary-cholesterol hyper-absorbers.

nodes.label: H-41 - The standard critiques of nutritional-cohort epidemiology are manageable, so well-conducted cohorts can support policy-relevant causal dietary inference
nodes.title: Residual confounding, dietary measurement error, small effect sizes, and reverse causation are manageable design/analysis problems rather than fatal flaws, so well-conducted prospective cohorts — triangulated with mechanistic/metabolic evidence and intervention trials of intermediate endpoints — can support causal dietary inference with high confidence for policy when hard-endpoint randomized trials are impractical.

nodes.label: H-42 - Eating eggs (-=2 yolks) raises TMAO production, feeding egg choline into the TMAO pathway
nodes.title: Habitual egg consumption, at least at >=2 yolks per meal, raises trimethylamine-N-oxide production, so egg-derived choline measurably enters the purported atherogenic TMAO pathway.

nodes.label: H-43 - The egg-induced TMAO rise may be clinically benign because it is postprandial and unaccompanied by inflammatory-oxidative changes
nodes.title: The TMAO rise from eating eggs may not translate into meaningful cardiovascular harm, because it is an acute postprandial excursion (not a fasting steady-state increase), it saturates above ~4 yolks, and it was not accompanied by any rise in inflammation (hsCRP) or LDL oxidation.

nodes.label: H-44 - An intermediate egg intake of about 3 to 6 per week minimizes CVD and mortality risk with harm at both lower and higher intake
nodes.title: The relationship between habitual egg intake and CVD/mortality risk is U-shaped with an optimum around 3-<6 eggs/week; both markedly lower (<1/week) and higher (>=10/week) habitual intake carry elevated risk in Chinese adults.

nodes.label: H-45 - TMAO is a non-causal marker not itself atherogenic in humans
nodes.title: Elevated plasma TMAO is not itself atherogenic in humans: it is a non-causal marker (of impaired renal clearance, gut dysbiosis, or an unhealthy background dietary pattern) rather than a cause of cardiovascular disease, so the choline->TMAO findings do not establish a causal harm pathway in people.

nodes.label: H-46 - Early egg introduction does not causally prevent allergy
nodes.title: Early controlled egg introduction is safe/tolerated but does not causally prevent egg allergy in high-risk infants: the lower allergy incidence in trials is not a true tolerogenic causal effect (e.g. it reflects trial selection, differential dropout of reacting infants, or would not generalize beyond the eczema-controlled protocol).

nodes.label: H-47 - Egg satiety is fully explained by protein content
nodes.title: Eggs do not increase satiety beyond what their protein and energy content explain: any greater reported fullness is accounted for by protein/energy load, with no egg-specific (food-matrix or micronutrient) satiety effect.

nodes.label: H-48 - Dietary choline is not an essential nutrient
nodes.title: Dietary choline is not an essential nutrient for healthy humans: endogenous de novo synthesis (via PEMT), together with usual methionine and folate intake, suffices, so a choline-free diet does not reliably produce deficiency and egg-borne choline is nutritionally dispensable.

nodes.label: H-49 - Some other overall egg-CVD relationship
nodes.title: The overall CVD/all-cause-mortality effect of moderate habitual egg intake is something other than a single population-wide protective, neutral, harmful, or U-shaped relationship -- e.g. there is no single population-average effect because the sign is genuinely subgroup/region-dependent.

nodes.label: H-5 - Eggs increase satiety and reduce hunger beyond their protein content
nodes.title: Eggs increase satiety and reduce hunger to a degree not explained by their protein content alone, plausibly aiding appetite/weight control.

nodes.label: H-50 - Some other origin of regional heterogeneity
nodes.title: The between-region variation in the egg-CVD association arises in some way other than genuine population effect-modification or healthy-user/residual confounding of the Asian inverse -- e.g. differential exposure ranges, differential measurement quality, or chance/publication artefact.

nodes.label: H-51 - Some other egg-T2D structure
nodes.title: The habitual-egg / incident-type-2-diabetes relationship has a structure other than the region-dependent-effect and the pure-confounding accounts -- e.g. a uniform modest causal effect (positive or inverse) present across all regions.

nodes.label: H-52 - Some other net egg effect in diabetics
nodes.title: In people with (pre)diabetes or metabolic syndrome, habitual egg intake has a net cardiometabolic effect other than clear safety, clear harm, or the specific glycemic-benefit/LDL-worsening trade-off above -- e.g. net benefit, or harm confined to a subgroup or endpoint not captured here.

nodes.label: H-53 - Some other egg lipid-profile effect
nodes.title: Habitual egg / egg-cholesterol intake affects the atherogenic blood-lipid profile in some way other than a modest linear LDL/apoB rise, a comparator-relative LDL worsening, or a net-neutral-to-HDL-favourable response -- e.g. effects confined to LDL particle quality or to specific subgroups.

nodes.label: H-54 - Some other operative exposure
nodes.title: The operative exposure behind the egg-CVD association is something other than egg-derived dietary cholesterol or accompanying dietary fat quality -- e.g. a non-cholesterol egg component, or no real operative exposure at all (the association being an artefact of dietary-pattern confounding).

nodes.label: H-55 - Some other responder structure
nodes.title: Inter-individual cholesterol responsiveness to dietary cholesterol has a structure other than a discrete hypo/hyper split, a feedback-regulation-driven continuum, or an ApoE-unidentifiable trait -- e.g. it is dominated by a different measured genetic/microbiome determinant, or is largely measurement noise and regression to the mean.

nodes.label: H-56 - Some other egg-TMAO relationship
nodes.title: Habitual egg intake relates to systemic TMAO in some way other than a form-limited negligible rise, no fasting rise, a renal-impairment-only rise, a real production rise, or a benign postprandial excursion -- e.g. a meaningful chronic steady-state elevation with clinical import in ordinary people.

nodes.label: H-57 - Some other egg-health characterization
nodes.title: Egg-health is characterised in some way other than an intrinsic fixed property, full comparator-contingency, substitution-indexing, or background-diet-indexing -- e.g. it is partly intrinsic and partly contingent in a way none of the above captures.

nodes.label: H-58 - Some other egg-Salmonella account
nodes.title: Intact Grade A shell eggs relate to the Salmonella enteritidis epidemic in some way other than being its major, internally (trans-ovarian) contaminated vehicle -- e.g. transmission is dominated by external/shell contamination controllable by handling, or eggs are not the principal vehicle.

nodes.label: H-59 - Some intermediate epistemic standing
nodes.title: The causal-inference standing of nutritional-cohort egg-health associations is something other than 'cannot support causal claims at all' or 'can support policy-grade causal inference' -- e.g. they support weak/qualitative causal claims for large effects only, an intermediate position.

nodes.label: H-6 - Moderate egg consumption causally lowers CVD risk in Chinese adults
nodes.title: Habitual egg consumption at a moderate level (up to ~1 egg/day) causally lowers cardiovascular disease risk in Chinese/Asian adults.

nodes.label: H-7 - Inverse egg-CVD association in China Kadoorie reflects healthy-user confounding not causation
nodes.title: The inverse egg-CVD association in this cohort is produced by residual and healthy-user confounding (daily egg consumers having higher socioeconomic status and healthier lifestyles) rather than a causal protective effect of eggs.

nodes.label: H-8 - Egg-T2D association is region-population-dependent - positive in US, null in Europe, null-to-inverse in Asia
nodes.title: The association between habitual egg consumption and incident type 2 diabetes is region/population-dependent: modestly positive (~14-18% per egg/day) in US/Western cohorts, null in Europe, and null-to-inverse in Asia.

nodes.label: H-9 - US egg-T2D association reflects residual confounding by the co-consumed Western dietary pattern, not a causal egg effect
nodes.title: The egg-T2D association reflects confounding by the co-consumed dietary pattern rather than a causal effect of eggs: in the US, egg intake marks adherence to a diabetogenic Western pattern (red/processed meat, refined grains, sugary drinks) that adjustment cannot fully remove, producing the positive association, whereas where eggs track prudent/traditional diets (e.g. Japan) the residual association is null-to-inverse.

nodes.label: H-13 - Egg consumption is a marker of the Western dietary pattern that drives T2D, not a causal factor
nodes.title: Egg consumption is a marker of a broader dietary pattern rather than a causal factor for T2D: in US studies eggs track a Western pattern (red/processed meat, fried potatoes, high-fat dairy, sweets, refined grains) associated with T2D, whereas in the Japanese study eggs track prudent/traditional patterns - so the residual association reflects the pattern, not the egg.

nodes.label: H-14 - Egg-T2D association is positive in US populations but null in non-US (European and Japanese) populations
nodes.title: The association between egg consumption and incident T2D is population-specific: positive in US cohorts (HR ~1.18 per 3 eggs/week) but null in non-US (European and Japanese) cohorts.

nodes.label: H-25 - In Japanese-Asian populations habitual egg consumption does not raise T2D risk
nodes.title: In Japanese (Asian) populations, habitual egg consumption is not associated with - and plausibly does not raise - the risk of type 2 diabetes, in contrast to the positive association seen in US cohorts.

nodes.label: H-32 - Higher egg consumption raises CVD risk specifically in people with pre-existing T2DM, not in non-diabetics
nodes.title: Habitual egg consumption raises cardiovascular disease risk specifically in people with pre-existing type 2 diabetes, while having no effect in non-diabetic individuals - i.e. diabetes status modifies the egg-CVD relationship.

nodes.label: H-37 - Moderate habitual egg intake up to one per day is neutral for CVD and mortality across income levels globally
nodes.title: Habitual egg consumption at moderate levels (up to ~1 egg/day) neither increases nor decreases cardiovascular disease or mortality risk, and this neutrality holds across low-, middle- and high-income countries and diverse diets.
